description: Figure 10 displays the true stress (σ_t)-strain (ε_t) curves (solid lines) superimposed over the strain-hardening rate (dσ/dε)-true strain curves (dashed lines) for Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic low-density steel samples subjected to hot-rolling reductions of 45% (HR45, black/grey), 68% (HR68, red), and 82% (HR82, blue). The solid lines show flow stress, while the dashed lines reveal a three-staged strain-hardening evolution for all samples: an initial drop after yielding (Stage I), a gradual rise to a peak (Stage II), and a continuous decrease until fracture (Stage III). The arrows mark interrupted true strains (ε_c ≈ 0.1, 0.2, and 0.4) where tensile tests were stopped, as stated in the caption. Visually, the strain-hardening rate remains at a high level near 2000 MPa across a broad plastic strain window, and the curves shift upward with increasing rolling reduction. The accompanying text reports that this distinct three-stage response, unlike the continuously decreasing trend in conventional high-SFE fcc metals, sustains high work-hardening capacity and thereby contributes to the superior strength-ductility combination.
